nodes.context: A street and prison gang founded in Chicago in 1952 as Simon City, later renamed Simon City Royals in 1969. Involved in drug trafficking, racketeering, and loan sharking.
Original greaser gang that formed in 1952 and later became Simon City Royals; continued as a separate gang until mid-1970s.
A gang that merged with Simon City to form the Simon City Royals in 1969.
A Chicago gang involved in turf wars with Simon City in the 1960s; later part of the Almighty Family faction with the Royals.
A Chicago gang that fought wars with the Simon City Royals in the 1970s and 1980s.
A white gang in Chicago that allied with the Simon City Royals in 1975 after their leader was killed by the Gaylords.
A white gang in Chicago that had a tenuous alliance with the Royals in the 1970s, later became rivals.
A Chicago gang that fought wars with the Simon City Royals in the 1970s and 1980s.
A Chicago gang that fought wars with the Simon City Royals in the 1970s; later part of the Almighty Family faction.
A Chicago gang that fought a war with the Southport and Fullerton Royals in the 1970s.
A Chicago gang led by Larry Hoover; allied with Simon City Royals in the Folk Nation alliance formed in 1978.
An alliance of Black, Latino, and White gangs created in 1978 by Larry Hoover, including the Simon City Royals.
A Chicago gang that killed a Simon City Royals leader in the early 1980s; later targeted by the Royals.
A Chicago gang that were former allies of the Simon City Royals but later feuded with them in the early 1990s.
A Chicago gang that were former allies of the Simon City Royals but later feuded with them in the early 1990s.
A Chicago gang that is part of the Almighty Family faction with the Royals.
A Chicago gang that is part of the Almighty Family faction with the Royals.
A prominent gang in Mississippi; listed as a rival of Simon City Royals in the infobox.
One of the four most prominent gangs in Mississippi, alongside Simon City Royals.
An alliance of gangs; listed as a rival of Simon City Royals in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: the new gang would now be called Simon City Royals . Now that they had given themselves a new name, the SCRz had officially separated themselves from Simon City, which continued to operate as a separate gang until the mid 1970s
edges.context: Simon City Royals split from Simon City, which continued as a separate gang.

edges.evidence_quote: An agreement was made and the new gang would now be called Simon City Royals
edges.context: Simon City merged with Ashland Royals to form Simon City Royals.

edges.evidence_quote: Simon City became involved in turf wars with other gangs including the Imperial Gangsters
edges.context: Simon City (predecessor) fought turf wars with Imperial Gangsters in the 1960s.

edges.evidence_quote: The Royals also fought major 'wars' with the Latin Kings
edges.context: Simon City Royals fought major wars with Latin Kings in the 1970s.

edges.evidence_quote: the Popes decided to form an alliance with the Royals
edges.context: Popes allied with Simon City Royals in 1975 after Gaylords killed Popes' leader.

edges.evidence_quote: the gang had tenuous alliances to the city's two major white gangs, the Popes and the Gaylords
edges.context: Simon City Royals had a tenuous alliance with Gaylords in the 1970s.

edges.evidence_quote: The Royals also fought major 'wars' with the ... Insane Deuces
edges.context: Simon City Royals fought major wars with Insane Deuces in the 1970s.

edges.evidence_quote: The Royals also fought major 'wars' with the ... Latin Eagles
edges.context: Simon City Royals fought major wars with Latin Eagles in the 1970s.

edges.evidence_quote: The Southport and Fullerton Royals were involved in a 'war' with the Insane Unknowns
edges.context: A faction of Simon City Royals (Southport and Fullerton) fought a war with Insane Unknowns.

edges.evidence_quote: Larry Hoover ... approached numerous gangs, including the Simon City Royals and together they created the Folk Nation
edges.context: Simon City Royals allied with Gangster Disciples to form Folk Nation in 1978.

edges.evidence_quote: together they created the Folk Nation
edges.context: Simon City Royals co-founded the Folk Nation alliance in 1978.

edges.evidence_quote: the leader of the Central Park and Wilson Simon City Royals ... was killed by the Puerto Rican Stones
edges.context: Puerto Rican Stones killed a Simon City Royals leader in the early 1980s.

edges.evidence_quote: they had begun to feud with their former allies, the Spanish Cobras
edges.context: Simon City Royals feuded with former allies Spanish Cobras in the early 1990s.

edges.evidence_quote: they had begun to feud with their former allies, the ... Latin Disciples
edges.context: Simon City Royals feuded with former allies Latin Disciples in the early 1990s.

edges.evidence_quote: This faction consists of the Royals, Imperial Gangsters, Harrison Gents, Latin Eagles and Ambrose.
edges.context: Simon City Royals are part of the Almighty Family faction with Harrison Gents.

edges.evidence_quote: This faction consists of the Royals, Imperial Gangsters, Harrison Gents, Latin Eagles and Ambrose.
edges.context: Simon City Royals are part of the Almighty Family faction with Ambrose.
